sourceMovie.title: Burn After Reading
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Burn After Reading (2008) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Annie Hall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Burn After Reading clicked for you, Annie Hall (1977) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Burn After Reading?
faq.answer: Try Annie Hall, I Care a Lot, The Squid and the Whale—each shares DNA with Burn After Reading while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Burn After Reading?
faq.answer: Burn After Reading (2008) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Burn After Reading worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
